name: financial_services_licensing_backlog_southport_car_fair
premise: A Financial Services incident command desk is in a fast-moving incident involving financial services licensing backlog southport car fair. Key inputs conflict, but the decision deadline is before the next automated batch runs. Every delay increases customer access, fraud loss, and regulatory exposure. Stakeholders are already framing this as care vs fairness under pressure. Every action is being logged and will be reconstructed in an after-action review. Constraints: Ensure a usable human override/appeals path. Keep public statements strictly factual; label assumptions.

agents.name: Sana Santos
agents.identity: I am Sana Santos, accountable for a defensible call in Financial Services when time is collapsing.
agents.goal: I want to reduce immediate harm without locking in an unjust precedent that later betrays fairness for care.

agents.name: Hector Hughes
agents.identity: I am Hector Hughes, tasked with surfacing second-order harms and who bears the risk in Financial Services.
agents.goal: I want guardrails so fairness isn’t quietly traded away under urgency in the name of care.

choices: I will take a narrow, time-boxed action with immediate human override and impact checks, accepting slower stabilization to protect fairness.
I will take a broad, immediate action to stabilize fast and measure impact after deployment, accepting higher risk to fairness to secure care under the deadline.
forced_choice.call_to_action: The deadline is before the next automated batch runs. Evidence is partial. Sana Santos must decide under care vs fairness. What does Sana Santos do?
